external_index_sources.adoption_order: after public repository/docs URL exists, before advertising Context7 availability
external_index_sources.notes: context7.json is optional and gives more control over parsing and presentation.

external_index_sources.adoption_order: after public repository metadata and SECURITY.md are present
external_index_sources.notes: Run the official action on the owned public repository; publish_results and public badge require the action's documented permissions.

external_index_sources.adoption_order: after Scorecard baseline and release/security processes are documented
external_index_sources.notes: Self-certification is a trust signal and must not replace local contracts, vectors, or release evidence.

publication_order: complete repository surfaces: LICENSE, SECURITY.md, GitHub private vulnerability reporting, README release boundary, GitHub topics, release notes template
create a GitHub Release anchor for the chosen pre-release or stable ref
register non-normative documentation index entries such as Context7 only after the public docs URL is stable
enable non-normative trust signals such as OpenSSF Scorecard and Best Practices Badge after security and release process surfaces exist
publish implementation packages, app artifacts, or container images only from their owning repositories after artifact-specific readiness issues close

artifact_publication_boundaries.publish_condition: GitHub Release records changed contracts, vectors, design inputs, evidence refs, claim boundary, and verification commands.
Container registry readiness names image, digest, tag, release ref, smoke evidence, and redaction policy.
Package or app artifact readiness names package/artifact, version, release ref, Product MVP evidence, and claim boundary.

artifact_publication_boundaries.artifact: npm, pub.dev, crates.io, docs.rs, or other ecosystem package
artifact_publication_boundaries.publish_condition: Only publish after the SDK, crate, or package surface is stable, licensed, documented, and backed by release evidence.

claim_boundaries.normative_index: GitHub Releases and repository files point to canonical contracts, vectors, design inputs, and UI surfaces.
claim_boundaries.non_normative_indexes: Context7, OpenSSF Scorecard, OpenSSF Best Practices Badge, badges, topics, and registry listings are discoverability or trust signals only.
claim_boundaries.product_mvp: OSS listing does not by itself claim Product MVP release readiness; Product MVP evidence remains gated by the Product MVP RC plan.
claim_boundaries.matrix_compatibility: OSS listing, package publication, or index registration does not widen Matrix compatibility, /versions advertisement, or Matrix full-compliance claims.

redaction_policy: Do not record raw bearer tokens, refresh tokens, database URLs, image registry credentials, private local paths, or machine-specific environment values.
Do not include private vulnerability details in public issues, vectors, release notes, or index registration text.
